title: Restore Missing High-Bits from Binary Array

statement.markdown:
Your task is to implement a function `solve` that takes an integer array `nums` as input, where each element in the array has a value between 0 and 2^20 - 1, inclusive. The function should find the minimum number of operations to restore all missing high-bits in the array. Note that a high-bit is considered missing if at least one element in the array has the high-bit set, but more than half of elements do not have the high-bit set. You should return the minimum number of operations required to restore all missing high-bits.

Here are some examples: 

* `solve([1,2,4])` should return `0` because there are no missing high-bits in this case.

* `solve([2,4,8])` should return `4` because there are two missing high-bits in this case.

* `solve([0,0,0])` should return `1` because the high-bit is missing in this case.

Note that the array can be non-empty and can contain negative numbers.

You can assume that the input array is not empty and the size of the array will not exceed `10000`.
statement.inputFormat: Input contains a single integer array `nums`.
statement.outputFormat: Your program should output the minimum number of operations required to restore all missing high-bits.
statement.notes: The time complexity of your program should be O(n) and the space complexity should be O(1).
